Gerresheimer Non-GAAP EPS of Є0.46, revenue of Є520.1M; initiates FY25 outlook

data.title: AI race: OpenAI said to cut down testing time for new models
data.description:
OpenAI has cut down the time and resources needed for identifying and mitigating risks while testing its artificial intelligence models, as pressure mounts to speed up new model launches amid intensifying competition, the Financial Times reported.

Employees and third-party groups have now been given days to evaluate the risks and performance of OpenAI's latest large language models, compared to several months previously.

Eight people familiar with the testing processes told FT that the tests have become less thorough. "We had more thorough safety testing when (the technology) was less important," said one of the sources, who's currently testing OpenAI's upcoming o3 model. "Because there is more demand for it, they want it out faster." 

The Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)-backed startup is pushing to release the o3 model as early as next week, giving less than a week for some testers to evaluate the model. In contrast, testers had six months to conduct safety checks for the GPT-4 model, which was launched in 2023.

Daniel Kokotajlo, a former OpenAI researcher who now leads nonprofit research group AI Futures Project, said AI companies are not required by law to inform the public about safety risks. "And they're under lots of pressure to race each other, so they're not going to stop making them more capable." 

OpenAI said its models are thoroughly tested and mitigated for safety, and the reduction in testing time is because of efficiencies made in its evaluation processes. "We have a good balance of how fast we move and how thorough we are," said Johannes Heidecke, the startup's head of safety systems.

data.title: Basel Medical to buy Singapore-based Bethesda Medical
data.description:
Basel Medical Group (NASDAQ:BMGL) has announced a definitive agreement to acquire Bethesda Medical, a Singapore-based healthcare provider specializing in diagnostic imaging and outpatient care.

The deal, expected to be completed around April 30, 2025, represents a strategic expansion in Singapore and Southeast Asia healthcare market.

An agreement between Basel Medical Group and Bethesda Medical strengthens their ongoing partnership, aiming for enhanced patient care and more efficient operations.

Combining Basel Medical Group's orthopaedic expertise with Bethesda Medical's diagnostic and outpatient services will foster a seamless referral pathway, enhancing patient access to specialized treatments and boosting clinical results and operational efficiency for both groups.

"The addition of Bethesda Medical is just the first step in our fast-paced growth strategy, with more developments to come," said Raymond Cheung, Group Chief Executive Officer of Basel Medical Group.

data.title: Google lays off at Android, Pixel, Chrome amid restructuring - report
data.description:
Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) Google laid off hundreds of employees from its Platforms and Devices division, which oversees key products such as Android software, Pixel smartphones, and the Chrome browser, The Information reported, citing a source with direct knowledge of the matter.

"Since combining the Platforms and Devices teams last year, we’ve focused on becoming more nimble and operating more effectively, and this included making some job reductions in addition to the voluntary exit program that we offered in January," a Google spokesperson said, according to the report. However, the company also noted that hiring continues across the globe and in the U.S.

data.title: Immuron exceeds AUD$5 million in sales in a fiscal year
data.description:
• Immuron (NASDAQ:IMRN) exceeded AUD$5 million in sales in a fiscal year - the highest sales in history, the company said on Thursday.
  • Sales in March quarter AUD$1.3 million, up 2% on prior year.

data.title: Gold tops $3,200/oz for first time as Trump tariffs trigger global recession fears
data.description:
Gold prices (XAUUSD:CUR) hit a record high in Asian trade on Friday, as the escalation in the U.S.-China trade war prompted demand for the safe-haven asset.

The yellow metal clocked a 6% gain this week, outpacing all other metals, as investors piled into bullion and the yen amid fears of a worldwide recession.

Spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) was up 0.5% at $3,204.23 an ounce, after hitting an all-time peak of $3,219.84 earlier in the session. Spot silver (XAGUSD:CUR) gained 0.5% to $31.33.

The dollar index (DXY), meanwhile, headed for a third weekly drop, with the Bloomberg Dollar Spot Index down 1.5% overnight, its biggest plunge in three years.

 

"Risks and uncertainty remained high despite Trump announcing a 90-day pause on the reciprocal tariffs," Daniel Hynes Senior Commodity Strategist with the ANZ said.

President Trump said that tariffs on Chinese imports are now at least 145%, which includes both a 125% rate that covers reciprocal duties as well as levies imposed on China for retaliating on US import taxes.

Investors also shrugged off data which showed U.S. inflation cooled in March.

Gold, traditionally seen as a hedge against uncertainty and inflation, has risen more than 22% this year.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Lucid wins bankruptcy auction to acquire Nikola’s Arizona factory and other assets
data.description:
Electric vehicle maker Lucid Group (NASDAQ:LCID) has won a bid to acquire select facilities and assets in Arizona previously belonging to now-bankrupt electric trucking company Nikola (OTC:NKLAQ).

According to the court filing, Lucid committed around $30 million in cash and non-cash considerations in exchange for Nikola’s manufacturing facility in Coolidge, Arizona; its Coolidge and Phoenix HQ leases; and certain machinery, equipment and inventory.

These facilities collectively add more than 884,000 square feet to Lucid's Arizona footprint. In addition, Lucid plans to offer employment to more than 300 former Nikola employees in roles across its Arizona facilities.

The transaction does not include the acquisition of Nikola's business, customer base, or technology related to Nikola's hydrogen fuel cell electric trucks.

The agreement follows Nikola's bankruptcy auction, which concluded on April 10, as part of its filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy relief.

data.title: Spain's inflation drops to 2.3% in March
data.description:
• Inflation rate in Spain decreased to 2.30% in March from 3% in February, meeting consensus estimates.

  • The Consumer Price Index increased 0.10% in March over the previous month.

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWP), (NASDAQ:EUFN)
